title: alnus incana var. tomophylla
intro: Alnus incana var. tomophylla, also known as Tomentose Green Alder, is a variety of the Green Alder tree, native to certain regions of Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and astringent properties, which can be beneficial in treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Alnus incana var. tomophylla include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and aiding in the treatment of digestive problems such as diarrhea and ulcers. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
